title: Total Wellness
catalogDescription: Explores wellness and provides students with the information necessary to make informed choices and decisions regarding physical activity, diet/weight control, nutrition, death and dying, recognizing and reducing stress, relaxation techniques, relationships, environmental issues, and risk factors for disease. Taught by a team of instructors.

title: Food and Health
catalogDescription: Explores diet and its relationship to the health and vitality of the individual. Provides practical information necessary for evaluating nutritional needs, obtaining essential nutrients, gaining strategies for improving dietary intake, practicing food safety, and understanding food labeling. Also focuses on diet and weight control, body image, and eating disorders, along with diet and disease.

title: Standard First Aid/CPR
catalogDescription: American Red Cross requirement for First Aid and CPR certification may be met. Covers adult, child, and infant first aid, CPR, rescue breathing, medical emergencies, bandaging, splinting and transporting.

title: Independent Study-Health
catalogDescription: For any student wishing to develop a basic knowledge in health when there is no offering in the regular curriculum.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.
